question.text: Under Section 19A of the Depositories Act, what is the minimum penalty for failure to furnish information, documents, or books to SEBI?

answer.text: Not less than ten lakh rupees, extending to ten lakh rupees per day, subject to a maximum of ten crore rupees
Not less than fifty thousand rupees, extending to fifty thousand rupees per day, subject to a maximum of fifty lakh rupees
Not less than five lakh rupees, extending to five lakh rupees per day, subject to a maximum of five crore rupees
Not less than one lakh rupees, extending to one lakh rupees per day, subject to a maximum of one crore rupees

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 19A of the Depositories Act provides that failure to furnish information, documents, books, returns or report to SEBI, or filing false/incorrect/incomplete information, shall attract a penalty which shall not be less than one lakh rupees but which may extend to one lakh rupees for each day during which such failure continues, subject to a maximum of one crore rupees. **The Trap:** The same penalty structure (Rs. 1 lakh minimum, Rs. 1 lakh per day, maximum Rs. 1 crore) applies to multiple types of violations under Sections 19A, 19B, 19C, 19D, and 19F. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Most penalties under the Depositories Act follow the same structure: Rs. 1 lakh minimum, Rs. 1 lakh per day continuing, Rs. 1 crore maximum. The exception is Section 19FA for unfair business conduct.
